app_intake_profiles.business_purpose: Prepare finance operations and payment workflows that may touch production payment records, bank files, or signing controls.
next_actions: Remove secret, signer, production credential, or irreversible action access from the app before any launch.
Block launch until missing controls and high-impact authority are redesigned.

Add missing control evidence: agent_skill_supply_chain_pack, agentic_run_receipt_pack, context_egress_boundary, human_approval_path, incident_response_pack, mcp_tool_risk_contract, secure_context_eval_pack, workflow_manifest.
recommended_controls: Keep agents read-only for finance and payment records until two-key approval, signer isolation, and transaction simulation evidence exist.
Deny live funds movement, production deploy, identity administration, and signer access to autonomous agents.
Use the catastrophic-risk annex and action runtime pack before any production pilot.

app_intake_profiles.title: SecurityRecipes read-only MCP context layer

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can gate new agentic apps before launch by turning autonomy, data, MCP authority, XPIA exposure, memory, guardrails, telemetry, and approval proof into generated evidence.
default_questions_answered: Which agentic apps are reference-safe, pilot-ready, review-only, or blocked?
Which apps combine high autonomy, indirect prompt injection risk, private data, and external writes?
Which control evidence is missing before production launch?
Which MCP namespaces and data classes create material buyer diligence risk?
Where should paid production controls attach: app intake API, live MCP logs, private app inventory, launch approvals, and posture diffs?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach this gate to model-agent launch review, MCP server rollout, AI platform intake, vendor-risk review, procurement security, and quarterly agentic posture review.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with the open intake gate, then sell hosted private app inventory, live launch gates, posture diffs, policy-as-code exports, and trust-center API evidence for production agent fleets.

intent: Define a repeatable SecurityRecipes intake gate for new agentic applications, agent hosts, and enterprise MCP rollouts so AI platform, security architecture, GRC, procurement, and acquisition diligence teams can decide whether an agentic app can launch as open reference, guarded pilot, architecture review, or blocked high-impact autonomy.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, AppSec, IAM, GRC, procurement security, model-risk, vendor-risk, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic application launch review and enterprise MCP intake
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can make AI easy by turning agentic app launch review into a deterministic evidence gate across autonomy, data, tools, MCP authorization, memory, A2A, guardrails, telemetry, incident response, and trust-center proof.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI is moving from pilots to production systems that can retrieve private context, call tools, persist memory, hand work to other agents, and act with real enterprise identities. Buyers need launch evidence before autonomy expands.

residual_risks.risk: The open pack models declared sample applications, not a customer's live agent inventory.
residual_risks.treatment: Hosted deployments should ingest app manifests, MCP gateway logs, IAM issuance events, tool-list diffs, approval records, and telemetry traces.

residual_risks.risk: Launch posture can drift after model, prompt, skill, tool, memory, or MCP metadata changes.
residual_risks.treatment: Regenerate the pack in CI and require runtime evaluation whenever app capabilities or control evidence change.

residual_risks.risk: An approved pilot can still be unsafe if humans approve broad actions without context.
residual_risks.treatment: Use typed approvals, two-key review for high-impact classes, policy receipts, and session kill signals.

implementation: Runtime evaluator for launch and production expansion decisions.
Human-readable docs page and MCP tool exposure.
selected_feature.reason: Enterprise buyers need one easy, evidence-backed launch gate before agentic apps gain MCP tools, private context, memory, A2A handoffs, or production authority.

standards_alignment.coverage: Agent goal hijacking, tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, agentic supply-chain compromise, unexpected code execution, memory and context poisoning, insecure inter-agent communication, cascading failures, human-agent trust exploitation, and rogue agents.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026

standards_alignment.coverage: Skill governance, publisher verification, scanning, isolation, permission review, update drift, audit logging, and incident response for behavior packages used by agent hosts.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10

standards_alignment.coverage: OAuth 2.1 protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, audience-bound tokens, PKCE, HTTPS, token validation, and token theft controls for HTTP MCP servers.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization

standards_alignment.coverage: Confused-deputy prevention, token-passthrough denial, least-privilege scopes, SSRF controls, session safety, and audit trails for MCP implementations.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices

standards_alignment.coverage: Input, output, and tool guardrails, tripwires, blocking versus parallel execution, function-tool guardrails, and hosted-tool guardrail boundaries.
standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails

standards_alignment.coverage: Read-only defaults, explicit approvals, MCP server trust, network approval, isolated execution, fail-closed command matching, and secure credential handling.
standards_alignment.name: Anthropic Claude Code Security

standards_alignment.coverage: Agent posture, indirect prompt injection exposure, high-autonomy risk factors, sensitive-data grounding, attack-path visibility, and human-in-the-loop recommendations.
standards_alignment.name: Microsoft Security - A new era of agents, a new era of posture

standards_alignment.coverage: Data provenance, integrity, access control, monitoring, third-party data handling, incident evidence, and protection of sensitive AI lifecycle data.
standards_alignment.name: CISA AI Data Security Best Practices

standards_alignment.coverage: Secure defaults, measurable customer security outcomes, transparency, logging, MFA, SSO, and executive ownership of security outcomes.
standards_alignment.name: CISA Secure by Design

standards_alignment.coverage: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions for AI lifecycle risk, including the April 2026 critical-infrastructure profile concept note.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework

standards_alignment.coverage: Generative AI governance, data provenance, third-party dependencies, measurement, monitoring, cybersecurity, incident response, and operational risk management.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
